sections.paragraph:
καταφεύγω fut. καταφεύξομαι LXX; 2 aor. κατέφυγον; aor. mixed 1 sg. κατέφυγα (JosAs 13:1) (s. φεύγω; Eur., Hdt.+).

① to get away from an area, with implication of having a destination in mind, flee εἰς τὰς πόλεις τῆς Λυκαονίας Ac 14:6 (cp. κ. εἰς τ. πόλ. Aeneas Tact. 1794; Lev 26:25; Dt 4:42; TestJob 17:5.—Jos., Bell. 6, 201, Ant. 18, 373).

② to gain shelter from danger, take refuge (PSI 383, 15 [248 b.c.]; PMagd 25, 8; Alex. Aphr., Fat. 32, II 2 p. 204, 26 to Asclepius; Stephan. Byz. s.v. Σύβαρις: κ. ἐπὶ τὴν θεόν=to the deity; Philo; πρὸς σέ JosAs 13:1) w. inf. foll. οἱ καταφυγόντες κρατῆσαι τῆς προκειμένης ἐλπίδος we who have taken refuge, to seize the hope that is placed before us Hb 6:18.—M-M. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aeneas Tact Aeneas Tact , IV b.c.—List 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 529
